According to {{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}}, p. 2, the average Dulok stands 1.35 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.</ref>

{{Quote|Get them!|translated=1|King Gorneesh orders his diamond-marked underling to stop the Ewoks aboard the battle wagon|<ref name="WW" />}}
As a member of [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh's]] [[Gorneesh's tribe|tribe]] from the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], '''one Dulok''' distinguished [[Gender/Legends|himself]] from the other members of his [[Species/Legends|species]] via a [[Color/Legends|yellow]] diamond shape emblazoned on his torso. He was otherwise a typical green-[[Hair/Legends|furred]] [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]], although he sometimes wore a white [[Clothing/Legends|cloth]] around his left [[Limb/Legends|arm]] and another on his right knee. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Dulok had become a [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] in his tribe, a position that allowed him to participate in assignments for King Gorneesh. These missions targeted the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], a [[City/Legends|settlement]] near the [[Dulok Swamp]]. In one instance, the diamond-marked Dulok rode aboard the [[Ewok battle wagon]] after his tribe stole it from the Ewoks—although the Dulok fighter fell off the vehicle during an [[Second battle of the Soul Trees|assault]] on the Ewoks' [[Soul Tree]]s, items of great religious importance to the Duloks' rivals. On another occasion, the Dulok accompanied others in a [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] expedition, only to be defeated by the rogue Ewok [[Asha/Legends|Asha]] and her companions.

==Biography==
===Attack on the Soul Trees===
[[File:DulokDiamond-WicketsWagon.jpg|thumb|left|The Dulok warrior's overzealous attack on the Ewok battle wagon landed him on the forest floor.]]
As a member of a [[Gorneesh's tribe|tribe]] of [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] led by [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]], one Dulok [[Gender/Legends|male]] distinguished himself from his fellows via a [[Color/Legends|yellow]] diamond shape marked onto the [[Hair/Legends|fur]] of his torso. Like the other males who lived in the [[Dulok Swamp]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], the diamond-emblazoned Dulok participated in various tasks set out for him by the King.<ref name="WW" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="EwoksDate">{{EwoksDate|appearance of the [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]]|[[Wicket's Wagon]]" and "[[Asha (episode)|Asha]]}}</ref> he joined several tribemates as they mounted a siege weapon known as a [[Ewok battle wagon|battle wagon]], which they had stolen from its [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] creators. In a bid to [[Second battle of the Soul Trees|destroy]] the Ewoks' [[Soul Tree]]s, natural [[Tree/Legends|trees]] that were of great spiritual significance to the Ewoks, the Dulok rode on the contraption's top deck as his comrades steered it to the forest around the Ewoks' home [[City/Legends|settlement]], [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Before the battle wagon could reach the site, however, a pair of Ewok [[Youngling/Legends|children]]—[[Malani]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]]—boarded the vehicle and attempted to stop the Dulok attack. When the Ewoks reached the top level, Gorneesh ordered the Duloks present to stop them. After [[Unidentified Dulok (O)|another Dulok]] charged the enemies and fell to the forest floor, the diamond-marked Dulok followed suit and inadvertently fell off the wagon. In the end, the Ewoks disabled the device, a move that saved their Soul Trees and forced the Duloks to retreat to their swamps.<ref name="WW" />

===Facing the Red Ghost===
That [[Winter/Legends|winter]], the diamond-marked Dulok participated in a [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] party with three others—a [[Unidentified Dulok (X)|Dulok]] marked with an ''X'', one marked with an ''O'', and [[Unidentified Dulok hunter|one]] with no markings—as they stalked a horned, bipedal creature known as a [[maramu]]. With [[Club/Legends|club]] in [[Limb/Legends|hand]], the Dulok with the diamond marking on his chest helped chase the maramu to the edge of a precipice, only to fall back down the slope when the the ''X''-marked Dulok stumbled and fell backward into his compatriots. They clambered back up the slope and netted the creature, but a savage howl sounded from the trees nearby. Then a red-furred figure—called by the Duloks "the Red Ghost," but actually a lone Ewok named [[Asha/Legends|Asha]]—appeared and snagged one of the Duloks with a [[Rope/Legends|lasso]], pulled him into the tree, and dropped him onto his fellow hunters. Spooked by the apparition, the diamond-marked Dulok and his companions fled.<ref name="A" />

He next followed King Gorneesh and the [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Umwak]], along with his fellows with the ''X'' and ''O'', the unmarked Dulok, and [[Unidentified Dulok (arrows)|another member]] of the tribe with [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]] marked on his chest, as they pursued quarry through the winter [[Water/Legends|snows]]. The group captured several small creatures, which they placed in boxes, and another maramu, which the diamond-marked Dulok helped subdue with ropes. As if from nowhere, the Red Ghost appeared and casually strode into the Duloks' midst to free the maramu and other creatures from their traps. On Gorneesh's orders, the Duloks with the ''X'', ''O'', and diamond marked on them captured the Ewok with lassos, but she grabbed their ropes, spun the Duloks in a circle, and flung them into a snowbank. The King ordered another attack, but the Ewok stepped aside, and the Duloks collided with one another. She then roped a Dulok with arrows marked on him and flung him into the pile of downed foes. The hunting party fled the scene and stumbled onto a hollow log, which the diamond-marked Dulok sat astride with his fellows as they slid down the snowy slope and off the side of a cliff. A body of water broke their fall, but the King swore not to hunt in the forest again, lest he once more face the wrath of the Red Ghost.<ref name="A" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:DuloksOXDiamond-Asha.jpg|thumb|right|The diamond-marked Dulok joined other members of his tribe as a hunter.]]
The diamond-marked Dulok served his tribe as a [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]]<ref name="WW" /> and hunter. He was familiar with the use of clubs, lassos,<ref name="A" /> and [[Spear/Legends|spears]]. He was ambidextrous, sometimes wielding weapons in his left hand,<ref name="WW" /> others in his right. He was particularly adept with the lasso, able to capture a fleeing maramu from a distance. Nevertheless, he was a bit clumsy; in one instance, he and his fellows collided with one another while pursuing an enemy.<ref name="A" /> While riding the Ewok battle wagon, he so zealously attacked his Ewok enemies that he overestimated his own momentum and fell to the forest floor.<ref name="WW" />

The diamond-marked Dulok served King Gorneesh loyally, doing the ruler's bidding with little hesitation. The Dulok, like many of his compatriots, took delight in inflicting pain and suffering on others. When his hunting party captured a female Ewok, the diamond-marked Dulok laughed with the others at the Ewok's plight.<ref name="A" />

A yellow diamond shape on his torso distinguished the Dulok from the other members of his tribe. In addition, he tied a strip of white [[Clothing/Legends|cloth]] across his left upper arm and another around his right knee. Otherwise, he appeared virtually identical to the other members of his tribe, with green fur, gray skin, brown [[Eye/Legends|eyebrows]], pink eyes, snarly [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and a lanky frame that stood him about 1.35 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.<ref name="A" /><ref name="height" />

==Behind the scenes==
The [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated television series introduced the Dulok character with the diamond shape on his torso with the [[November 9]], [[1985]], airing of "[[Wicket's Wagon]]," an episode written by [[Paul Dini]] and directed by [[Raymond Jafelice]]. The Dulok was later featured in the [[November 30]], 1985, episode, "[[Asha (episode)|Asha]]," also by Dini and Jafelice.<ref name="SWC 66">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref> The [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] feature film ''[[Tales from the Endor Woods]]'', first released in [[2004]], includes edited versions of both episodes.<ref name="TFTEW">''[[Tales from the Endor Woods]]''</ref> Adaptations of the stories<ref name="WWb">[[Wicket's Wagon (book)|''Wicket's Wagon'' picture book]]</ref><ref name="TRG">''[[The Red Ghost: An Ewok Adventure]]''</ref> and supplemental sources indirectly mention the Dulok by referencing the hunting parties and war bands in which he participated.<ref name="SWC 66" /><ref name="EGC x">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref><ref name="CSWE I 44">{{CSWECite|I|44|Asha}}</ref>
